title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, was convicted of raping a 20-year-old woman in a Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023.
Jammal received a 13.5 to 15-year state prison sentence for rape, followed by 5 years probation for assault.
The court noted Jammal's actions were driven by a 'shocking sense of entitlement,' with no influence from intoxication.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Historic Newton church to become condos after city approves conversion of Church of the Open Word on Highland Ave.
Developer Habitech Communities plans to create six church condos and a new five-story building with 26 units.
This redevelopment reflects a trend of converting churches into housing amid shrinking congregations and increased housing demand.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Kawhi Leonard is a potential trade target for the Celtics, offering versatility and defensive strength.
LeBron James, entering free agency, could be a fit for Boston's lineup if he leaves the Lakers.
John Collins, Jaxson Hayes, and Coby White are additional options to bolster the Celtics' roster depth.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series by defeating Molloy University in a three-game series.
Endicott College secured its fourth consecutive Division III World Series appearance after victories over Salve Regina University.
Bentley and Endicott will compete in North Carolina and Ohio respectively; their opponents and schedules are yet to be announced.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Celtics plan to work out UConn's Alex Karaban ahead of NBA Draft next month, reports CT Insider.
Karaban, a Southborough native, ranks No. 32 on ESPN’s top 100 prospects; Boston holds picks No. 27 and No. 40.
Karaban averaged 12.5 points in 151 games, leaving UConn as their all-time leader in wins and three-pointers.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall in Boston on May 22 as part of his 'Whatever's Clever! World Tour.'
Puth's concert blended jazz and pop, featuring songs from his new album and older hits, connecting with the audience's nostalgia.
The tour showcases Puth's musical ingenuity with live improvisations and insights into music theory, reflecting his deep musicianship.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Jakob Dylan and the Wallflowers performed 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full at The Cabot in Beverly.
The Wallflowers paired their set with Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' album, performing it track by track.
Dylan's vocals were gruffer but expressive, enhancing the performance of both Wallflowers' and Petty's songs.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston's population decreased by 1,338 in a year, marking a decline of 5,644 since 2020.
Everett gained 2,362 residents and Worcester added 1,916, showing the state's largest population increases.
Massachusetts' population grew by 15,500 residents, with slowed growth due to high costs and reduced immigration.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments and Market Basket's parent company are on Forbes' list of largest US family businesses.
Fidelity ranked 14th, earning $37.7 billion annually; Demoulas Super Markets ranked 69th with $8 billion revenue.
Forbes ranks multigenerational companies with significant family ownership; Fidelity is led by Abigail Johnson.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities for a campus housing overhaul to modernize residential life.
The development aims to maintain affordability while enhancing student amenities and aligning with academic facilities.
Project addresses enrollment growth and housing shortages, implementing phased renovations to keep students on campus.
